summary: Joel Spolsky makes a brief promotional post tying Mother's Day to a common tech experience: fixing your mom's computer. He suggests removing spyware, installing Firefox, and cleaning up toolbars, then announces that Fog Creek Copilot (their remote desktop product) will be free for the day to make remote tech support easy.
key_points: Frames a product promotion around a universally relatable experience for tech-savvy people: being the family IT support
Identifies the common PC problems of the era: spyware, adware, unwanted toolbars, and browser issues
Promotes Fog Creek Copilot by making it free for Mother's Day, lowering the barrier to trying the product
money_quotes: This Sunday is Mother's Day. Why not fix your mom's computer?
You know: remove the spyware and adware, install Firefox, and make it so that weird toolbar toast doesn't pop up every 15 seconds.
key_insight: A clever product promotion that leverages the universal experience of being your family's unpaid IT department to drive trial usage of a remote desktop tool.
